message:
fix(codegen): Store callable in temp var to avoid labeled block call syntax

When generating assertRaises code, attribute accesses and call nodes
can produce labeled blocks (e.g., __m_getattr: { ... }). Directly
calling these with () is invalid Zig syntax.

Fix: For .attribute and .call nodes, store the result in __callable
temp var first, then call it:

  Before (invalid): _ = __m_getattr: { ... }() catch |err| ...
  After (valid):    const __callable = __m_getattr: { ... };
                    _ = __callable() catch |err| ...

This fixes the "expected ';' after statement" error that was causing
42 compile failures in @core1 tests.
